steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The word but sets up a contrast: until recently, Theia had a certain status, but now researchers claim to have found actual pieces of it inside Earth. The blank must describe Theia before physical evidence existed — when it was only a theoretical idea.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'notional': means existing as an idea rather than something real — fits perfectly; Theia was only a theoretical construct before any fragments were found. ✓
  • Choice B — 'spurious': means false or fake — the text doesn't say Theia was invented or fraudulent. ✗
  • Choice C — 'veritable': means genuine or real — opposite of what's needed; the blank describes the period before physical evidence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'desultory': means lacking purpose or connection — unrelated to the idea-vs-reality contrast. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
